summary.goal: Implement a properly structured macOS menu bar following HIG conventions with standard menus, keyboard shortcuts, and context-aware enable/disable
summary.description:
Every macOS app needs a properly structured menu bar. Even with minimal UI, the menu bar must follow HIG conventions.

## App Menu ([App Name])
- About [App Name]
- ---
- Settings... (Cmd+,)
- ---
- Hide [App Name] (Cmd+H)
- Hide Others (Cmd+Option+H)
- Show All
- ---
- Quit [App Name] (Cmd+Q)

## File Menu
- Open... (Cmd+O) -- opens standard macOS file dialog filtered to .md
- Open Recent -> submenu (macOS manages this automatically via NSDocumentController)
- ---
- Close Window (Cmd+W)

## Edit Menu
- Copy (Cmd+C)
- Select All (Cmd+A)
- ---
- Find -> submenu (Stretch goal -- not in v1, but menu structure should accommodate it)

## View Menu
- Actual Size (Cmd+0) -- reset font size to default
- Zoom In (Cmd+Plus)
- Zoom Out (Cmd+Minus)
- ---
- Enter Full Screen (Cmd+Ctrl+F)

## Window Menu
- Minimize (Cmd+M)
- Zoom
- ---
- Bring All to Front
- [List of open windows]

## Help Menu
- [App Name] Help (placeholder, can link to documentation or be empty for v1)

## Design Decisions
- **No Format menu**: This is a viewer, not an editor
- **No Edit > Paste/Cut/Undo**: Read-only app has no use for these
- **Find (Cmd+F)**: Deliberately omitted from v1 for simplicity. The View menu should be designed to accommodate it in v2.
- **Open Recent**: Leverage NSDocumentController's built-in recent files management. Even though we don't persist state, the system tracks recent documents automatically.
- **Print**: Deliberately omitted from v1. Not part of the minimal viewer concept. Can be added later via File > Print (Cmd+P).

## HIG Compliance
- Menu structure follows Apple Human Interface Guidelines ordering
- Standard keyboard shortcuts for all system-level actions
- Menu items enable/disable based on context (e.g., Copy disabled when nothing selected)
- Menus use system font, not app theme font
- Menu bar is always visible (not hidden/auto-hidden)

acceptance_criteria.description: App, File, Edit, View, Window, Help menus present with standard HIG ordering
All standard keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+O, Cmd+W, Cmd+Q, Cmd+comma, Cmd+Plus/Minus) functional
Open Recent submenu populated via NSDocumentController

observations: Standard NSMenu structure with HIG ordering
Menu items context-aware: enable/disable based on state
Find deliberately omitted from v1, structure accommodates v2
Six menus: App, File, Edit, View, Window, Help
File menu: Open, Open Recent, Close Window
Edit menu: Copy, Select All (+ Find placeholder for v2)
View menu: Actual Size, Zoom In/Out, Full Screen
System font for menus (not theme font)

steps.title: Create main menu bar structure programmatically
steps.description: Create the entire menu bar programmatically in AppDelegate (not via Interface Builder/storyboard, since we have no storyboard). Build NSMenu with 6 top-level menus: App, File, Edit, View, Window, Help — in standard HIG order. Each menu with its items and keyboard shortcuts. The App menu includes About, Settings (Cmd+,), Hide, Hide Others, Show All, Quit. Wire each menu item to the appropriate action selector. Use NSApplication standard actions where possible (hide:, unhideAllApplications:, terminate:).

acceptance_criteria.description: 6 top-level menus in HIG order: App, File, Edit, View, Window, Help
All standard keyboard shortcuts assigned and functional
App menu: About, Settings, Hide, Hide Others, Show All, Quit
Menu bar created programmatically, no storyboard dependency

steps.title: Implement File menu with Open, Open Recent, Close
steps.description: Build the File menu: (1) Open... (Cmd+O) — triggers NSOpenPanel via WindowManager; (2) Open Recent submenu — managed automatically by NSDocumentController; (3) separator; (4) Close Window (Cmd+W) — closes the key window. For Open Recent, ensure NSDocumentController.shared is properly configured and noteNewRecentDocumentURL is called when files are opened. The Close Window item should use the standard performClose: action.

acceptance_criteria.description: Open... (Cmd+O) triggers file open dialog
Open Recent submenu shows previously opened files
Close Window (Cmd+W) closes the frontmost window
Menu items in correct order with separators

architecture.recent: NSDocumentController manages Open Recent submenu automatically

steps.title: Implement Edit menu with Copy and Select All
steps.description: Build the Edit menu with only the items relevant to a read-only viewer: (1) Copy (Cmd+C); (2) Select All (Cmd+A). Deliberately omit Paste, Cut, Undo, Redo — these are editing operations. Use the standard copy: and selectAll: actions which NSTextView responds to automatically. Add a separator after Select All to leave space for a future Find submenu (v2). Copy should be disabled when no text is selected (NSTextView handles this via validateMenuItem).

acceptance_criteria.description: Copy (Cmd+C) and Select All (Cmd+A) present and functional
No Paste, Cut, Undo, Redo items
Copy disabled when no text is selected
Separator after Select All for future Find submenu

architecture.validation: validateMenuItem: automatically disables Copy when no selection

steps.title: Implement View menu with zoom controls and full screen
steps.description: Build the View menu: (1) Actual Size (Cmd+0) — resets font size to default; (2) Zoom In (Cmd+Plus) — increases font size; (3) Zoom Out (Cmd+Minus) — decreases font size; (4) separator; (5) Enter Full Screen (Cmd+Ctrl+F) — uses standard toggleFullScreen: action. Wire zoom items to ThemeManager font size adjustment methods. Full screen uses the standard NSWindow action and works with macOS full screen, split view, and Stage Manager automatically.

acceptance_criteria.description: Actual Size (Cmd+0), Zoom In (Cmd+Plus), Zoom Out (Cmd+Minus) present
Enter Full Screen (Cmd+Ctrl+F) uses standard toggleFullScreen:
Zoom items wire to ThemeManager font size adjustment
Full screen works with split view and Stage Manager

steps.title: Implement Window and Help menus
steps.description: Build the Window menu with standard items: (1) Minimize (Cmd+M) — performMiniaturize:; (2) Zoom — performZoom:; (3) separator; (4) Bring All to Front — arrangeInFront:; (5) automatic window list (macOS populates this automatically when NSApp.windowsMenu is set). Build the Help menu with a placeholder: [App Name] Help — can link to documentation or be a no-op for v1. Set NSApp.helpMenu so macOS handles it correctly.

acceptance_criteria.description: Window menu with Minimize, Zoom, Bring All to Front
Open windows automatically listed in Window menu
Help menu with placeholder item
NSApp.windowsMenu and NSApp.helpMenu set correctly

architecture.window_menu: Set as NSApp.windowsMenu for automatic window list population
